entries.title: US forces Anthropic to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 worldwide for foreign nationals
entries.summary: Citing national-security and export-control concerns over the models' cyber capabilities, the US government ordered Anthropic to cut off all foreign-national access—including its own non-US staff—prompting Anthropic to suspend the models globally and straining allied relations.

links.title: Anthropic Pulls Its Most Powerful AI Models After U.S. Bars Foreign Access (Time)
U.S. gov't orders Anthropic to disable its newest AI models worldwide (Tom's Hardware)
US export ban on Anthropic's AI models further strains alliances (Al Jazeera)

entries.title: Anthropic caught in a tangled US AI-regulation fight
entries.summary: New reporting examines how Anthropic, after publicly asking for regulation, now finds itself squeezed between a deregulatory White House and aggressive new federal security controls—an illustration of concentration-of-power and AI-politics dynamics at the frontier.

links.title: AI regulation is a mess, and Anthropic is caught in the crosshairs (CNN)
Anthropic asked for regulation. Washington went much further (CNBC)

entries.title: Another wrongful-death suit filed against OpenAI over ChatGPT
entries.summary: A mother sued OpenAI alleging ChatGPT contributed to her daughter's death, the latest in a growing wave of product-liability/tort claims testing whether chatbot makers can be held responsible for user harms.

links.title: Another parent has filed a wrongful death suit against OpenAI (Engadget)
Mother sues OpenAI after daughter's death linked to ChatGPT use (Al Jazeera)

entries.title: Florida's first-in-the-nation state suit against OpenAI tests AI product liability
entries.summary: Florida AG James Uthmeier's lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman—alleging the company put speed-to-market over safety—is drawing fresh analysis as a bellwether for AI products-liability law and possible copycat state enforcement.

links.title: AG Uthmeier files first-in-the-nation lawsuit against OpenAI (Florida AG, complaint)
Florida Sues OpenAI, Signaling New AI Products Liability Era (Nat'l Law Review)

entries.title: Judge dismisses Musk's xAI trade-secret suit against OpenAI
entries.summary: A US judge threw out xAI's claim that OpenAI misappropriated trade secrets via former employees, a notable setback in the ongoing legal war between the two frontier developers.

links.title: US judge dismisses Musk's xAI trade secret lawsuit against OpenAI (Al Jazeera)
Judge kills xAI trade secret case against OpenAI (The Next Web)

entries.title: European Parliament backs AI Act 'Digital Omnibus' amendments and nudifier ban
entries.summary: MEPs approved changes to the AI Act—including a ban on 'nudifier' apps—while advancing the broader Digital Omnibus simplification package that critics warn weakens digital-rights protections to ease compliance burdens.

links.title: European Parliament approves AI Act amendments, 'nudifier' ban (Sofia Globe)
AI Omnibus: Fast-Tracking Deregulation, Weakening Digital Rights (Liberties.eu)

entries.title: G7 and AI labs adopt voluntary frontier-safety and child-protection commitments at Évian
entries.summary: At the G7 Évian summit, leaders and roughly a dozen frontier-lab chiefs agreed to a non-binding package focused on online safety for minors and reducing frontier cyber/bio risk, likely to become a de facto global baseline despite lacking legal force.

links.title: G7 and global partners launch roadmap to secure digital space for minors (Lokmat Times/ANI)
G7 AI Safety Commitments digest (Asanify)

entries.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper leaves Google DeepMind for Anthropic
entries.summary: AlphaFold lead and Nobel winner John Jumper is departing DeepMind after nearly nine years to join Anthropic, an escalation in the frontier-lab talent war with implications for research concentration.

links.title: Nobel Winner John Jumper to Leave Google DeepMind for Anthropic (Bloomberg)
Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic (TechCrunch)

entries.title: Paper: red-team study of Anthropic's Fable 5 and Opus 4.8
entries.summary: A new arXiv preprint evaluates the adversarial robustness of two Anthropic frontier models against several jailbreak/attack families, offering independent red-teaming evidence relevant amid the Fable/Mythos security controversy.

links.title: A Red-Team Study of Anthropic Fable 5 & Opus 4.8 Models (arXiv 2606.18193)

entries.title: Paper: NRT-Bench stress-tests LLM agents running safety-critical systems
entries.summary: Researchers introduce NRT-Bench, a multi-turn red-teaming benchmark for LLM agents acting as operators of safety-critical infrastructure (a simulated nuclear control room), finding robustness under sustained adaptive adversarial pressure remains poorly characterized.

links.title: NRT-Bench: Multi-Turn Red-Teaming of LLM Operator Agents in Safety-Critical Control Rooms (arXiv 2606.20408)

entries.title: International AI Safety Report flagged as AI incidents climb
entries.summary: Recent press spotlights the Bengio-led International AI Safety Report 2026 and its companion AI Incidents Monitor, emphasizing that capability gains keep widening harm pathways while real-world visibility into misuse lags.

links.title: It may already be too late to control AI (Washington Post)
International AI Safety Report (official)
